Title: Associate Professional, Data Analytics
Description:
At Cotality, we are driven by a single mission—to make the property industry faster, smarter, and more people-centric. Cotality is the trusted source for property intelligence, with unmatched precision, depth, breadth, and insights across the entire ecosystem. Our talented team of 5,000 employees globally uses our network, scale, connectivity and technology to drive the largest asset class in the world. Join us as we work toward our vision of fueling a thriving global property ecosystem and a more resilient society.

Cotality is committed to cultivating a diverse and inclusive work culture that inspires innovation and bold thinking; it's a place where you can collaborate, feel valued, develop skills and directly impact the real estate economy. We know our people are our greatest asset. At Cotality, you can be yourself, lift people up and make an impact. By putting clients first and continuously innovating, we're working together to set the pace for unlocking new possibilities that better serve the property industry.

Job Description


Perform analytic tasks according to the established schedules to ensure timely delivery of solutions to clients, both internal and external.
Maintain documentation in accordance with established standards.
Provide support for inquiries from external clients and internal users; gather and share information needed to facilitate complete and accurate responses in a timely fashion.
Identify and escalate potential production-related issues; provide support to troubleshoot and resolve production-related issues.
Analyze data sets and log files as needed to support production operation functions, client inquiries, issue resolution, and project work.
Create data pipelines for enriched, aggregated, etc. datasets using SQL.
Create reports; research and analyze data and report trends and communicate vital information to management.
May identify opportunities to improve established processes, procedures, and standards for efficiency and compliance with current best practices and guidelines.


Job Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree required, with a concentration in mathematics, economics or statistics preferred.
0-5 years of directly related experience.
Strong data analysis, data quality, and/or data visualization skills required.
Experience with SQL and open source programming languages (Python, R) required.
Proficiency in Excel, Word, PowerPoint required.
Experience in data extraction, data manipulation, data visualization, and reporting preferred.
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau) strongly preferred.
Experience with non-Windows-based operating system (Linux) and cloud platforms (e.g. Google Cloud Platform) desired.
Demonstrated success in effective communication, both verbal and written required.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively and effectively with others in a functional organization required.
Organization, accountability, and attention to detail required.


Annual Pay Range

0 - 0

Application Window

This opportunity is expected to remain posted through the date identified below, subject to business needs.

Thrive with Cotality

At Cotality, we offer more than just a job, we provide a benefits experience designed to support your whole self. From a flexible working model to competitive time off and standout health coverage with meaningful perks and growth opportunities, our package is built to help you thrive at work and in life.

Highlights, Depending On Role Classification, Include


Time off: Generous PTO and 11 paid holidays, plus well-being and volunteer time off.
Family Support: Up to 16 weeks of fully paid parental leave and a baby stipend.
Health: Multiple medical plan options with mental health and wellness support offerings.
Retirement: 401(k) with company match and vesting after one year.
Financial Perks: $400 annual well-being stipend and tuition assistance up to $5,250.
Extras: Recognition Rewards, Referral bonuses, exclusive discounts and more!


Cotality is an Equal Opportunity employer committed to attracting and retaining the best-qualified people available, without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability or status as a veteran of the Armed Forces, or any other basis protected by federal, state or local law. Cotality maintains a Drug-Free Workplace.

Cotality is fully committed to a work environment that embraces everyone’s unique contributions, experiences and values. We offer an empowered work environment that encourages creativity, initiative and professional growth and provides a competitive salary and benefits package. We are better together when we support and recognize our differences.

Privacy Policy

Global Applicant Privacy Policy

By providing your telephone number, you agree to receive automated (SMS) text messages at that number from Cotality regarding all matters related to your application and, if you are hired, your employment and company business. Message & data rates may apply. You can opt out at any time by responding STOP or UNSUBSCRIBING and will automatically be opted out company-wide.

Connect with us on social media! Click on the quicklinks below to find out more about our company and associates

Title: Data Engineer (Entry- to Mid-level)
Description:
Meet Our Team

The Global Enterprise Data Management team at Pega is focused on Data Warehouse, Analytics and Data Governance. Our job is to take data and transform it into actionable, consumable, information that is utilized at the executive levels.




Please note we are unfortunately unable to sponsor or transfer visas, or work on OPT/CPT EAD at this time.




Picture Yourself At Pega

Data is the lifeblood of every business, particularly digital organizations. We're looking for an experienced Database Engineer to join our growing global team. You'll work with a diverse global team of Database Engineers, Analysts and Business Stakeholders to help Pega’s evolving Data and Analytics strategies.




What You'll Do At Pega

Design and implement enterprise Data Engineering solutions are agile and scale with Pega’s business growth
Ensure data models are designed for flexibility to support future business needs and enhancements.
Collaborate with the Data Engineering Team members across geographies to deliver on project requirements.
Help with the effort to create standards, processes, change controls for the Data Engineering team to ensure operational consistency.




Who You Are

You are a strong collaborator with the ability to communicate complicated information in a clear, concise, and actionable manner.
You are highly organized and enjoy working on several projects simultaneously with the ability to prioritize and meet deadlines.
You consider yourself to be adaptable and flexible while maintaining a high level of professionalism.




What You've Accomplished

Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Technology or equivalent
Overall, 2 to 5 years of IT experience
2+ years of Data Development experience
2+ ETL / Data Integration / Bigdata Development
1+ years Data Modeling, Data Cleansing, Data Profiling
Proficient in SQL Development
Python development adhering to industry standard best practices for data engineering
Big Data concepts (Streaming data to various apps from cloud platforms)
Knowledge on Big data technologies (Spark, HDFS, Kafka, any NoSQL DB)
Understanding of different structures of data like csv, excel, JSON, XML etc
Working knowledge on any one of the major cloud database technologies (GCP, AWS, Snowflake, Azure etc)
Working knowledge of RDBMS such as MSSQL, Oracle, SAP HANA




Pega Offers You

Gartner Analyst acclaimed technology leadership across our categories of products
Continuous learning and development opportunities
An innovative, inclusive, agile, flexible, and fun work environment
Competitive global benefits program inclusive of pay + bonus incentive, employee equity in the company
Opportunity to learn new technologies in a rapidly changing environment
Show more

Title: Data Entry Clerk
Description:
About The Company

PrideStaff is a reputable staffing and employment services company dedicated to connecting talented professionals with leading organizations across various industries. With a strong commitment to excellence, integrity, and personalized service, PrideStaff has built a trusted reputation for delivering quality staffing solutions that meet the unique needs of both clients and candidates. Our extensive network of employers and our focus on fostering long-term relationships enable us to provide meaningful career opportunities for job seekers while helping companies find the right talent to drive their success.

About The Role

We are currently seeking a detail-oriented and efficient Data Entry Clerk to join our team in San Jose, CA. This role is vital in ensuring the accuracy and integrity of data within our clients' systems. The ideal candidate will possess a minimum of six months of relevant data entry experience, demonstrate excellent typing skills, and have a keen eye for detail. The position offers a competitive hourly rate ranging from $22 to $28, with standard Monday through Friday working hours from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm. As a Data Entry Clerk, you will be responsible for transcribing information from various documents, maintaining accurate records, and supporting administrative functions essential to our clients' operational efficiency.

Qualifications

To be considered for this role, candidates should meet the following qualifications:


Minimum of 6 months of relevant data entry experience
Typing speed of at least 40 WPM and 6000 KSPH
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
Ability to multitask effectively in a fast-paced environment
Basic understanding of customer service protocols
Proficiency in using keyboards and data entry software
High school diploma or equivalent


Responsibilities

The primary responsibilities of the Data Entry Clerk include:


Reading and accurately transcribing information from various documents into digital formats
Completing subsequent data entries using standard keyboarding techniques
Logging activities and maintaining records of data entry tasks performed
Inputting both alphabetic and numeric information with precision
Storing and organizing documents in appropriate digital or physical locations
Ensuring data accuracy and integrity through regular review and validation
Collaborating with team members and supervisors to meet data management goals


Benefits

Working with PrideStaff offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your health, financial security, and overall well-being. Benefits include:


Medical, prescription, and wellness benefits
Dental and vision plan options
Short-term disability coverage
401(k) retirement plan with company contributions
Paid holiday and vacation time
Opportunities for career growth and skill development


Equal Opportunity

PrideStaff is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected class. We believe in providing equal employment opportunities to all candidates and employees, ensuring a respectful and supportive environment where everyone can thrive and succeed.

Title: Spontaneous Application
Description:
About Pathway

At Pathway we are shaking the foundations of artificial intelligence by introducing the world's first post-transformer model that adapts and thinks just like humans.

Our breakthrough architecture outperforms Transformer and provides the enterprise with full visibility into how the model works. Combining the foundational model with the fastest data processing engine on the market, Pathway enables enterprises to move beyond incremental optimization and toward truly contextualized, experience-driven intelligence. We are trusted by organizations such as NATO, La Poste, and Formula 1 racing teams.

Pathway is led by co-founder & CEO Zuzanna Stamirowska, a complexity scientist who created a team consisting of AI pioneers, including CTO Jan Chorowski who was the first person to apply Attention to speech and worked with Nobel laureate Geoff Hinton at Google Brain, as well as CSO Adrian Kosowski, a leading computer scientist and quantum physicist who obtained his PhD at the age of 20.

The company is backed by leading investors and advisors, including Lukasz Kaiser, co-author of the Transformer ("the T" in ChatGPT) and a key researcher behind OpenAI's reasoning models. Pathway is headquartered in Palo Alto, California.

Requirements

You are:


Willing to learn and grow in the job
Respectful of others
Fluent in English


Why You Should Apply


Opportunity to work on the ground floor of the ever-expanding AI industry close to some of the most groundbreaking innovations
Work closely with the Team and gain hands-on experience scaling a disruptive AI company
High visibility and impact roles
Diverse, multicultural environment


Benefits


Type of contract: permanent employment contract
Benefits: Employee Stock Option Plan
Location: Remote work. Possibility to work or meet with other team members in one of our offices: Palo Alto, CA; Paris, France or Wroclaw, Poland

Title: AI2 Data Science Co-op/Intern (FALL 2026)
Description:
Type De Rôle

Internship/Co-op

Session De Stage

Fall/Term 1

Lieu De Travail

Toronto, Ontario, Canada

Horaire

37.5

Détails De La Rémunération

$45,700 - $74,400 CAD

La TD a à cœur d’offrir une rémunération juste et équitable à tous les collègues. Les occasions de croissance et le perfectionnement des compétences sont des caractéristiques essentielles de l’expérience collègue à la TD. Nos politiques et pratiques en matière de rémunération ont été conçues pour permettre aux collègues de progresser dans l’échelle salariale au fil du temps, à mesure qu’ils s’améliorent dans leurs fonctions. Le salaire de base offert peut varier en fonction des compétences et de l’expérience du candidat, de ses connaissances professionnelles, de son emplacement géographique et d’autres besoins particuliers du secteur et de l’entreprise.

En tant que candidat, nous vous encourageons à poser des questions sur la rémunération et à avoir une conversation franche avec votre recruteur, qui pourra vous fournir des détails plus précis sur ce poste.

Department Overview

Description du poste :

Co-op and Internship opportunities allow you to gain valuable work experience across a number of the businesses at TD. You will work with experienced colleagues, receive world class training, and be part of a community of students across TD, where you will have an impact, grow as individual and experience our culture of care.

Role

Our Co-op/Intern Programming is offered with select Co-op and Internship roles and is designed to help you better understand the TD business, build on critical career capabilities, and broaden your professional network. This program is designed to complement your on-the job experience and features:


Engage in interactive sessions with key leaders across TD, gaining valuable insights into leadership and the organization’s vision.
Participate in learning sessions focused on innovation, personal branding, and fostering professional and personal growth.
Join a supportive community of students and professionals, expanding your network and building the skills needed for a thriving career in analytics and AI.


We want our co-ops and interns to have the TD and AI2 experience (Analytics Insights & Artificial Intelligence), where we develop talent, help build great careers and do what it takes to make our clients' lives easier! The goal for our co-ops and interns is that they can have a meaningful experience while growing their skills and gaining new perspectives. Chosen candidates will have the chance to learn and develop their competencies and build great careers.

Job Description

Responsible for interpreting data, producing insights, creating relevant collateral/ data visualization reports, and recommending actions on complex issues to support business needs. You will perform a variety of activities, which may include:


Perform Python/SQL data queries and support data sets for analysis/modelling, including cleaning data and feature engineering
Analyze large, spatially enabled data sets and identify trends and insights to inform business decisions; communicate findings to technical and non-technical audiences
Support team members and business partners to develop, test and deploy analytics solutions, for example: forecasting, optimization, or models
Proactively identify and solve for business problems and opportunities where a data-driven solution would be appropriate and develop proposals / approaches
Collaborate with business partners to support the business case development process for analytics solutions


Job Requirements


Currently enrolled in either a recognized undergraduate or graduate degree majoring in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, Science, Statistics, Business, Data Analytics, Business/Commerce or related field with the intent of going back to school at the start of your work term.
Must be enrolled in an undergraduate degree with the intent of going back to school at the start of your work term
Strong statistical and analytical skills and interest are necessary.
Strong proficiency in one or more of Python, SQL, or other data-related programming languages.
Knowledge in spatially enabled data, geospatial processing tools/platforms
Knowledge in one or more Azure, R, AWS, Databricks and Github is a plus
Knowledge in Tableau and/or PowerBI
Excellent verbal and written communication with the ability to work collaboratively and effectively in a team environment.


Additional Information


This position is a 4-month work term and will commence September 8 – December 18, 2026.
Applications must include a transcript, cover letter (one letter-sized page or less) and a resume (maximum of 2 pages).
We welcome all applications; however, we will only contact qualified candidates chosen for an interview. Thank you for your interest.
TD requires employees to reside in the country where the role is located, irrespective of remote working arrangements.
TD is committed to providing you with the best candidate experience and internship in these unique circumstances. As such, work location and start dates are subject to change.


HOURS

Monday-Friday, standard business hours

INCLUSIVENESS

At TD, we are committed to fostering an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel valued, respected and supported. Notre programme de rémunération globale reflète les investissements que nous faisons pour aider nos collègues et leur famille à atteindre leurs objectifs en matière de bien-être mental, physique et financier. La rémunération globale à la TD inclut le salaire de base, la rémunération variable et bien d’autres régimes clés, comme des avantages sociaux en matière de santé et de bien-être, des régimes d’épargne et de retraite, des congés payés, des avantages bancaires et des rabais, des occasions de développement de carrière et des programmes de récompenses et reconnaissance. En tant que candidat, nous vous encourageons à poser des questions sur la rémunération et à avoir une conversation franche avec votre recruteur, qui pourra vous fournir des détails plus précis sur ce poste.

Department Overview

Description du poste :

Co-op and Internship opportunities allow you to gain valuable work experience across a number of the businesses at TD. You will work with experienced colleagues, receive world class training, and be part of a community of students across TD, where you will have an impact, grow as individual and experience our culture of care.

Role

Our Co-op/Intern Programming is offered with select Co-op and Internship roles and is designed to help you better understand the TD business, build on critical career capabilities, and broaden your professional network. This program is designed to complement your on-the job experience and features:

  • Engage in interactive sessions with key leaders across TD, gaining valuable insights into leadership and the organization’s vision.
  • Participate in learning sessions focused on innovation, personal branding, and fostering professional and personal growth.
  • Join a supportive community of students and professionals, expanding your network and building the skills needed for a thriving career in analytics and AI.

We want our co-ops and interns to have the TD and AI2 experience (Analytics Insights & Artificial Intelligence), where we develop talent, help build great careers and do what it takes to make our clients' lives easier! The goal for our co-ops and interns is that they can have a meaningful experience while growing their skills and gaining new perspectives. Chosen candidates will have the chance to learn and develop their competencies and build great careers.

Job Description

Responsible for interpreting data, producing insights, creating relevant collateral/ data visualization reports, and recommending actions on complex issues to support business needs. You will perform a variety of activities, which may include:

  • Perform Python/SQL data queries and support data sets for analysis/modelling, including cleaning data and feature engineering
  • Analyze large, spatially enabled data sets and identify trends and insights to inform business decisions; communicate findings to technical and non-technical audiences
  • Support team members and business partners to develop, test and deploy analytics solutions, for example: forecasting, optimization, or models
  • Proactively identify and solve for business problems and opportunities where a data-driven solution would be appropriate and develop proposals / approaches
  • Collaborate with business partners to support the business case development process for analytics solutions

Job Requirements

  • Currently enrolled in either a recognized undergraduate or graduate degree majoring in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, Science, Statistics, Business, Data Analytics, Business/Commerce or related field with the intent of going back to school at the start of your work term.
  • Must be enrolled in an undergraduate degree with the intent of going back to school at the start of your work term
  • Strong statistical and analytical skills and interest are necessary.
  • Strong proficiency in one or more of Python, SQL, or other data-related programming languages.
  • Knowledge in spatially enabled data, geospatial processing tools/platforms
  • Knowledge in one or more Azure, R, AWS, Databricks and Github is a plus
  • Knowledge in Tableau and/or PowerBI
  • Excellent verbal and written communication with the ability to work collaboratively and effectively in a team environment.

Additional Information

  • This position is a 4-month work term and will commence September 8 – December 18, 2026.
  • Applications must include a transcript, cover letter (one letter-sized page or less) and a resume (maximum of 2 pages).
  • We welcome all applications; however, we will only contact qualified candidates chosen for an interview. Thank you for your interest.
  • TD requires employees to reside in the country where the role is located, irrespective of remote working arrangements.
  • TD is committed to providing you with the best candidate experience and internship in these unique circumstances. As such, work location and start dates are subject to change.

Monday-Friday, standard business hours

Title: Business Intelligence Developer
Description:
Company Description

URTM Solutions builds automation products that turn complex financial and operational data into scalable, decision-grade systems. We specialize in productized Business Intelligence for FP&A and industry-specific use cases, helping organizations move beyond spreadsheets and manual processes toward fully automated, reliable analytics.




Role Overview

We are looking for a Senior BI / Analytics Developer who brings together advanced Power BI and DAX expertise, strong financial and analytical thinking, and a clean, disciplined approach to data visualization.

This role goes beyond building reports. You will own, maintain, and continuously improve implemented analytics products, proactively ensuring accuracy, performance, and long-term reliability across clients and industries.




Responsibilities

Design, build, maintain, and evolve Power BI dashboards and semantic models as part of URTM’s automation products
Translate financial and business logic (FP&A, KPIs, variance analysis) into scalable, high-performance DAX models
Develop clear, executive-ready visualizations that support real decision-making
Proactively monitor and maintain implemented products, ensuring ongoing data accuracy, consistency, and performance
Perform data validation, reconciliation, and issue investigation to maintain decision-grade outputs
Implement and maintain row-level security (RLS) and governance standards
Support client implementations, including data onboarding, cleanup, and go-live
Collaborate closely with product, implementation, and sales teams to support scalable delivery




Qualifications

Advanced Power BI expertise, including data modeling, DAX, Power Query, and performance optimization
Proven experience designing and developing high-quality Power BI reports and dashboards
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex financial and operational datasets
Excellent problem-solving skills and high attention to detail
Strong communication skills, with the ability to clearly explain insights to non-technical stakeholders




Nice to Have

Experience implementing AI capabilities within Power BI projects
Exposure to Power Apps, automation workflows, or machine learning concepts
Experience working in productized or multi-client BI environments




How to Apply

Please include a Power BI portfolio (demo reports, screenshots, or links) as part of your application.

You can apply in either of the following ways:

Submit your application via our Careers page: https://urtmsolutions.com/careers/
Or email your resume and portfolio directly to: careers@urtmsolutions.com




We review every application and commit to responding within 3 business days to candidates selected for the next interview round.

Title: Editorial Specialist (Data Analysis Focus)
Description:
We are

Wizeline, a global AI-native technology solutions provider, develops cutting-edge, AI-powered digital products and platforms. We partner with clients to leverage data and AI, accelerating market entry and driving business transformation. As a global community of innovators, we foster a culture of growth, collaboration, and impact.

With the right people and the right ideas, there’s no limit to what we can achieve

Are you a fit?

Sounds awesome, right? Now, let’s make sure you’re a good fit for the role:

Key Responsibilities


Interpret, validate, and transform financial data into structured, high-quality outputs.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and compliance with internal standards.
Translate complex financial datasets into clear, concise, and well-structured content through light editorial writing.
Apply markup, formatting, and content structuring to prepare materials for publication.
Perform technical and editorial reviews of data-driven content.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (data, editorial, and operations) to maintain quality and consistency.
Identify and resolve inconsistencies in data and content presentation.
Support team development and mentoring, promoting best practices in both data handling and editorial quality.


Must-have Skills


3+ years of experience working with financial data, data analysis, or editorial/data-focused roles.
Strong ability to interpret, validate, and transform financial datasets.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (complex formulas, data cleaning, data modeling).
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, with strong grammar, syntax, and clarity.
Experience in copyediting, technical writing, or content quality review.
Exceptional attention to detail and ability to ensure high data and content accuracy.
Strong analytical and critical-thinking skills.


Nice-to-have


AI Tooling Proficiency: Leverage one or more AI tools to optimize and augment day-to-day work, including drafting, analysis, research, or process automation. Provide recommendations on effective AI use and identify opportunities to streamline workflows.
Understanding of fixed income fundamentals (yields, duration, credit).
Familiarity with financial data platforms such as Bloomberg Terminal or Morningstar.
Experience with Workfront or similar workflow management tools.


What We Offer


A High-Impact Environment
Commitment to Professional Development
Flexible and Collaborative Culture
Global Opportunities
Vibrant Community
Total Rewards
Specific benefits are determined by the employment type and location.


Find out more about our culture here.

Title: Staff Data Analyst, Servicing
Description:
For our client, we are seeking a Staff Data Analyst, Servicing to join the team of a leader in the Finance & Insurance space. This role will shape high-impact initiatives at the intersection of strategy, innovation, and execution. You will partner across technical and business teams to turn complex ideas into scalable solutions and meaningful outcomes. The position offers the opportunity to influence both roadmap priorities and long-term organizational capability.

Location: Remote - US based candidates only, no visa sponsorship available

Compensation: $157,000 – $217,500 annually

Responsibilities


Lead development of analytics to drive servicing efficiency improvements
Create executive-level presentations to communicate findings
Design and analyze experiments to test user interaction hypotheses
Collaborate with teams to enhance servicing data capabilities
Maintain business intelligence tools for reporting and decision-making
Mentor junior analysts and promote a culture of data excellence


Qualifications


8+ years in senior analytical roles within technology and finance
Strong proficiency in payments, product, or operations analytics, using SQL, Python, and/or R
Proven ability to derive actionable insights and tell analytical stories
Experience with large datasets and tools like Databricks, Looker, and Tableau
Degree in a quantitative field such as Economics, Statistics, or Data Science


Benefits


Competitive compensation and annual equity grants
Generous 401(k) with 2:1 company match up to $15,000
Affordable medical, dental, and vision coverage with high employer contribution
Paid parental leave and family-centered support benefits
Annual wellness allowance and productivity allowance
Employee Assistance Program for mental health support
Connection through team events and employee resource groups


Our client is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet every qualification—your background could be exactly what this team needs.

Title: Business Analyst, Intelligence
Description:
About The Company

HealthCentral is a pioneering health experience company dedicated to transforming how individuals understand and navigate life with chronic, serious, and rare conditions. Our mission is to empower patients and caregivers by providing accurate, clear, and accessible health information through innovative digital platforms. We blend lived experience with clinical expertise, ensuring that our content and tools are grounded in science while resonating with real-life experiences. Recognized as a Great Place To Work®, our company fosters a welcoming and inclusive environment that values collaboration, innovation, and continuous learning. By prioritizing our employees' well-being and professional growth, we strive to create a supportive culture where everyone can thrive.

About The Role

We are seeking a talented Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst to join our team in a full-time capacity. This role is pivotal in building and enhancing campaign performance data products within our healthcare media ecosystem. The BI Analyst will serve as a bridge between data, media, and storytelling, transforming complex, multi-source campaign data into insightful, decision-ready narratives for internal teams and clients. Reporting directly to the Chief Product Officer, the successful candidate will collaborate closely with Account Management, Product, and Analytics teams to standardize reporting processes, improve data pipelines, and evolve measurement strategies for healthcare professional (HCP) and direct-to-consumer (DTC) campaigns. This role offers an exciting opportunity to influence how data drives decision-making and campaign optimization in a fast-paced, health-focused environment.

Qualifications


1-3 years of experience in BI analytics, digital marketing analytics, or data-focused roles
Experience working with campaign performance or user engagement data
Proficiency with BI tools such as Sigma, Tableau, Looker, or similar platforms
Familiarity with analytics platforms like Adobe Analytics or equivalent
Working knowledge of data modeling concepts and metric standardization
Strong Excel skills for data analysis and reporting
Analytical mindset with the ability to generate insights and solve problems
Exceptional attention to detail ensuring data accuracy and consistency
Curiosity and initiative for system improvements and automation workflows
Excellent communication skills to translate data into clear narratives for stakeholders
Ability to create client-ready dashboards and deliverables
Strong cross-functional collaboration skills
Nice to have: experience in healthcare, pharma, or regulated industries, exposure to programmatic media, DSPs, ad tech ecosystems, basic SQL knowledge, familiarity with AI tools or automation workflows


Responsibilities


Own the development and maintenance of campaign performance data products, including dashboards, reporting frameworks, and insight layers
Translate complex, multisource data into clear, actionable insights and narratives that support decision-making
Identify performance drivers, trends, and opportunities across campaigns, content, and audiences to inform strategic adjustments
Advance reporting from descriptive to diagnostic and prescriptive insights to enhance campaign effectiveness
Design and maintain scalable BI dashboards for internal teams and clients, ensuring usability and clarity
Define and standardize core metrics, business logic, and calculation frameworks to ensure consistency
Integrate data from multiple sources such as Sigma, Adobe Analytics, and ad servers into a unified reporting layer
Pull and validate data from Sigma dashboards, ensuring accuracy and reliability
Collaborate with teams to incorporate data from sources like Adobe Analytics and ad servers, standardizing metrics and calculation rules
Support the transition from manual reporting workflows to automated, scalable systems
Assist in developing AI-assisted reporting workflows for enhanced insight generation
Ensure data quality, validation processes, and system reliability are continuously improved
Partner with product, analytics, and engineering teams to enhance data pipelines, integrity, and reporting tools
Embed optimization insights into scalable reporting and dashboard experiences for diverse stakeholders


Benefits

About The Role

We are seeking a talented Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst to join our team in a full-time capacity. This role is pivotal in building and enhancing campaign performance data products within our healthcare media ecosystem. The BI Analyst will serve as a bridge between data, media, and storytelling, transforming complex, multi-source campaign data into insightful, decision-ready narratives for internal teams and clients. Reporting directly to the Chief Product Officer, the successful candidate will collaborate closely with Account Management, Product, and Analytics teams to standardize reporting processes, improve data pipelines, and evolve measurement strategies for healthcare professional (HCP) and direct-to-consumer (DTC) campaigns. This role offers an exciting opportunity to influence how data drives decision-making and campaign optimization in a fast-paced, health-focused environment.

Qualifications

  • 1-3 years of experience in BI analytics, digital marketing analytics, or data-focused roles
  • Experience working with campaign performance or user engagement data
  • Proficiency with BI tools such as Sigma, Tableau, Looker, or similar platforms
  • Familiarity with analytics platforms like Adobe Analytics or equivalent
  • Working knowledge of data modeling concepts and metric standardization
  • Strong Excel skills for data analysis and reporting
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to generate insights and solve problems
  • Exceptional attention to detail ensuring data accuracy and consistency
  • Curiosity and initiative for system improvements and automation workflows
  • Excellent communication skills to translate data into clear narratives for stakeholders
  • Ability to create client-ready dashboards and deliverables
  • Strong cross-functional collaboration skills
  • Nice to have: experience in healthcare, pharma, or regulated industries, exposure to programmatic media, DSPs, ad tech ecosystems, basic SQL knowledge, familiarity with AI tools or automation workflows

Responsibilities

  • Own the development and maintenance of campaign performance data products, including dashboards, reporting frameworks, and insight layers
  • Translate complex, multisource data into clear, actionable insights and narratives that support decision-making
  • Identify performance drivers, trends, and opportunities across campaigns, content, and audiences to inform strategic adjustments
  • Advance reporting from descriptive to diagnostic and prescriptive insights to enhance campaign effectiveness
  • Design and maintain scalable BI dashboards for internal teams and clients, ensuring usability and clarity
  • Define and standardize core metrics, business logic, and calculation frameworks to ensure consistency
  • Integrate data from multiple sources such as Sigma, Adobe Analytics, and ad servers into a unified reporting layer
  • Pull and validate data from Sigma dashboards, ensuring accuracy and reliability
  • Collaborate with teams to incorporate data from sources like Adobe Analytics and ad servers, standardizing metrics and calculation rules
  • Support the transition from manual reporting workflows to automated, scalable systems
  • Assist in developing AI-assisted reporting workflows for enhanced insight generation
  • Ensure data quality, validation processes, and system reliability are continuously improved
  • Partner with product, analytics, and engineering teams to enhance data pipelines, integrity, and reporting tools
  • Embed optimization insights into scalable reporting and dashboard experiences for diverse stakeholders

Benefits

  • Competitive salary range of $65,000 - $75,000
  • Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance plans
  • Flexible remote working options, including NYC and hybrid arrangements
  • Generous paid time off including 21 days PTO, 6 Wellness Days, 2 Volunteer Days, and 2 Floating Holidays
  • 401(k) retirement plan with company match

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About The Company

Shape a brighter financial future with us. We are a next-generation financial services company and a national bank dedicated to transforming the way individuals interact with personal finance. Leveraging innovative, mobile-first technology, we serve millions of members, empowering them to reach their financial goals through tailored solutions and seamless digital experiences. Our industry is undergoing rapid change, and we pride ourselves on being at the forefront of this evolution. Our core values guide our daily operations, fostering a culture of innovation, integrity, and inclusivity. We are committed to making a meaningful impact on people's lives by providing high-quality financial products and services. Join us to invest in your career and contribute to redefining the future of finance.

About The Role

We are seeking an experienced and motivated Staff Data Engineer to join our Data Products team within the SIPS (Spend, Invest, Protect, Save) division, supporting all SoFi Financial Services. This role involves leading the design, development, and optimization of high-quality data models and pipelines that serve critical analytical, reporting, and machine learning functions across the organization. As a technical leader, you will shape the vision and strategy for building foundational data infrastructure that is highly leveraged, scalable, and reliable. Your expertise will help empower consumers to make data-driven decisions by providing high-availability, democratized data. The ideal candidate is a detail-oriented professional with a strong background in big data challenges, data modeling, and pipeline development, capable of working collaboratively across teams to deliver innovative solutions that meet business needs.

Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or a related field
8+ years of experience in data engineering and analytics technical strategy
Proficiency in data engineering tools and technologies: Snowflake, PostgreSQL, Python, SQL, GitLab, AWS, Airflow, DBT, and others
Experience with relational and cloud database platforms such as Snowflake, Redshift, or GCP
Hands-on experience managing end-to-end data engineering projects, including planning, execution, and resource management
Experience leveraging AI tools within data workflows, such as Cursor, Claude Code, CoPilot
Strong programming skills in Python and/or other data-centric languages
Thorough knowledge of data modeling, database design, data architecture principles, and data operations
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to simplify complex issues into actionable solutions
Experience in the Fintech industry is advantageous
Willingness to travel up to 10–15% as required


Responsibilities


Design and develop robust data models and pipelines to support data ingestion, processing, storage, and retrieval processes
Evaluate and select appropriate technologies, frameworks, and tools to build scalable, reliable, and efficient data infrastructure
Optimize data engineering systems and processes to handle large-scale datasets effectively, ensuring scalability both horizontally and vertically
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including data scientists, software engineers, and business stakeholders to gather requirements and deliver tailored data solutions
Communicate complex technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders to facilitate understanding and decision-making
Enforce data governance policies to maintain data integrity, security, and compliance with relevant regulations
Partner with data governance and security teams to implement data protection mechanisms and access controls
Stay updated with emerging data technologies and best practices to continually improve data infrastructure and workflows


Benefits


Competitive salary package aligned with experience and skills
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance plans
Retirement savings plans with company matching contributions
Paid time off and holiday leave
Opportunities for professional development and continuous learning
Inclusive and diverse work environment that fosters innovation
Flexible work arrangements where applicable
Employee assistance programs and wellness resources


Equal Opportunity

We are an equal employment opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic under applicable law. We promote a workplace where all employees can thrive and contribute to our mission. Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. We also provide reasonable accommodations to candidates with disabilities throughout the application and interview process.
